title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Can you buy term insurance for your family?
Meta description: Are you planning to buy a life cover for all the members in your family? Read this blog to find out if you can buy a term plan for your family.
Keywords: term life insurance, term insurance policy, term insurance premium
About term insurance
Term life insurance is a pure protection plan that offers a life cover to the insured person. This life cover is valid for a specific period, known as the policy term. In case the person passes away during the policy term, the insurer pays death benefits to the nominee mentioned under the plan.
In order to enjoy this life cover, the policyholder has to pay premiums to the insurer, as per the terms and conditions of the term insurance policy. This term insurance premium can be a one-time payment, or it can be paid periodically on a monthly, quarterly, semi-annual or annual basis, based on the policy’s features.
What does term insurance cover?
Generally, one term insurance plan covers one life. And it covers all kinds of death, with the only exception being suicide in the first year of the policy. For instance, a term insurance plan covers various kinds of death like death due to natural causes, accidents, medical illnesses and even COVID-19 subject to policy terms & conditions being met.
Do you need a cover for other members in your family?
Now that you know the details of what a term insurance covers and excludes, you may be wondering if it is enough to buy a term plan for you alone. In this day and age, there are many double income families where both the spouses are breadwinners. So, since both people contribute to the income of the household, if anything untoward happens to one of them, the family may find it financially stressful.
Let us look at an example to understand this better. Sanjay is a working professional, and so is his wife Aarti. Sanjay’s income is used to meet the family’s regular expenses, while Aarti’s income is used to pay off the family’s home loan EMIs. Now, if something happens to either of them, there is a loss of income for the family as a whole, and the surviving members will find it difficult to meet their financial life goals.
So, do you need a cover for other members in your family? The bottom line is, if there are other members in your family who contribute to the household income in any manner, you may consider buying a life cover for them.
You can either buy a different term plan for each working member, or you could make use of a joint term insurance plan to cover both you and your spouse under the same term insurance policy.
What is a joint term insurance plan?
A joint term insurance plan offers a life cover to you and your spouse under a single policy. The life cover remains valid for a specific term. Some joint term plans work on the principle of first death. Here, if one of the policyholders passes away before the other person, the insurer pays out the death benefits as specified under the term plan to the surviving policyholder. And in cases both the policyholders pass away simultaneously, the insurer pays out the benefits to the nominees.
In other joint term insurance plans, there is an individual cover on both the lives insured. So, for instance, each policyholder of a joint term insurance plan may have a cover of Rs. 50 lakhs.
.
Should you choose a joint term plan or multiple individual term plans?
To cover you and your spouse with life insurance, you may either choose to get individual term plans for each of you, or a joint term plan that covers both of you.
In the following cases, it may be better to choose a joint cover.
You and your spouse are in your late 30s or 40s,  the term insurance premiums for separate life covers may  be higher as opposed to a joint life policy where the premiums may be lower
Your lifestyles are similar, such as you and your spouse are both either smoker or non-smokers,
Note that in case of a joint life policy with individual covers, you may also be entitled to a discount in the premium depending upon the features of the product you choose.
Conclusion
Whatever the finer details may be, it remains that getting a term life insurance cover for your spouse and your family members is generally a preferredfinancial decision. It allows the other members in your family to enjoy a financial safety net and pursue their life goals without any trouble.

Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.

Conclusion Whatever the finer details may be, it remains that getting a term life insurance cover for your spouse and your family members is generally a preferredfinancial decision.
